channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Teen 'shore party' members in biggest Australian cocaine bust jailed
item.description: The pair, now aged 19, were sentenced with 18 months and two years in prison respectively.
item.dc:creator: Talissa Siganto

media:group.media:description: The teenagers will spend time behind bars for their role in the importation scheme.

item.title: Education watchdog launches new probe into 'concerning' school allegations
item.description: The Victorian education regulator is reinvestigating the elite alternative primary school at the centre of abuse allegations.
item.dc:creator: Josie Taylor and Coco Veldkamp

media:group.media:description: The Fitzroy Community School is under investigation by the Victorian Education watchdog.

item.title: Migration to be linked to housing availability under Coalition plan
item.description: The Coalition will link overseas migration to housing supply as part of its migration strategy, which would in effect slash the number of migrants allowed into the country.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Asher

media:group.media:description: Angus Taylor is looking to cap overseas migration.

item.title: Police told to switch bodycams to flight mode to avoid detection by criminals
item.description: Thousands of police officers have been advised to disable the bluetooth on their Axon body-worn cameras during raids and undercover jobs to avoid being detected and tracked, after Four Corners exposed a security flaw with the devices.
item.dc:creator: Grace Tobin

media:group.media:description: Four Corners exposed a security flaw in Axon tasers and body-worn cameras, which are used by police forces across Australia.

item.title: Ex-Sydney teacher spared jail after grooming child online
item.description: William Roberto Gulson was on Friday spared time behind bars but will be placed on the NSW Child Protection Register for eight years.
item.dc:creator: Jamie McKinnell

media:group.media:description: Former Sydney Knox Grammar teacher William Roberto Gulson was placed on the NSW Child Protection Register.

item.title: Two purged Chinese ex-defence officials sentenced to death with reprieves
item.description: Two former defence ministers have received their sentences after a long-running corruption crackdown targeting China's military.

media:group.media:description: Li Shangfu fell in Xi Jinping's anti-corruption purge that focused on the armed forces in recent years.

item.title: Trio found guilty of 'payback' murder over fake gun sale
item.description: Jury finds trio guilty of murder over the shooting death of Newcastle man Anthony Nugent over a fake gun deal in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Giselle Wakatama

media:group.media:description: Anthony Nugent was shot dead at his Hamilton South unit in September 2022.

item.title: Breaking: NT government promises child protection review after child death
item.description: The NT government is set to trigger a wide-reaching review of its child protection system, in the wake of the tragic death of five-year-old Kumanjayi Little Baby.
item.dc:creator: Matt Garrick

media:group.media:description: Robyn Cahill is the minister responsible for the department that oversees child protection in the NT.

item.title: Blanket 10km/h limit for e-bikes in Qld should be watered down, committee says
item.description: A proposed 10 km/h speed limit for e-mobility on footpaths should only apply in high pedestrian zones and not on shared paths, a Queensland parliamentary committee has recommended.
item.dc:creator: Alex Brewster

media:group.media:description: New laws have been proposed following an inquiry into e-mobility devices in Queensland.

item.title: Man jailed after burning down apartment block and running amok with axe
item.description: A man who burnt down an apartment block and terrorised a central Queensland town with an axe is jailed for six years, but will be eligible for parole in February with time already served in custody.
item.dc:creator: Liam McNally

media:group.media:description: Raymond Thomas Whyte pleaded guilty in the Mackay District Court.

item.title: Aussie teen returns from injury to race in new Italian championship
item.description: Teenager Aiva Anagnostiadis, a rising star of Australian motorsport, is hoping to leave a difficult end to 2025 in her rear view as she prepares for her newest challenge in Italy.
item.dc:creator: Michael Doyle

media:group.media:description: Aiva Anagnostiadis will be part of the inaugural GT4 Italy Series.

item.title: Charges dropped against Dion Prestia and Steven May over alleged brawl
item.description: Prosecutors have dropped all charges against current and former footballers Dion Prestia and Steven May over an alleged brawl in Sorrento on Victoria's Mornington Peninsula on Boxing Day in 2024.
item.dc:creator: Costa Haritos

media:group.media:description: Police have dropped charges against Dion Prestia (left) and Steven May over an alleged brawl.

item.title: What we know about renewed fighting between US and Iran
item.description: The US and Iran both blame each other for initiating renewed attacks as tensions in the Strait of Hormuz ratchet up, threatening the ceasefire.

media:group.media:description: Iran and the US have been wrestling for control of the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: One of Ipswich's biggest food charities forced to close
item.description: Amid strong demand for its services, an Ipswich food charity is being forced to close in a devastating blow for those in need.
item.dc:creator: Charlie McLean

media:group.media:description: Ipswich Foodbarn General Manager Qim Kauwhata says the food charity will open for the last time on Saturday.

item.title: Portrait of Iluwanti Ken by Richard Lewer wins $100,000 Archibald Prize
item.description: The Melbourne-based artist painted Pitjantjatjara elder Iluwanti Ken, who is known for her large-scale drawings and was also a finalist in this year's Wynne Prize.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Story

media:group.media:description: Richard Lewer has won the Archibald Prize 2026 with his portrait of Iluwanti Ken.

item.title: Flights axed as carrier faces soaring fuel costs and falling demand
item.description: Link Airways will suspend its Shellharbour–Melbourne and Shellharbour–Brisbane services from May 20, saying it is losing tens of thousands of dollars a day as fuel costs rise and passenger numbers fall.
item.dc:creator: Kelly Fuller and Melinda James

media:group.media:description: Link Airways will suspend flights from Shellharbour Airport from May 20.

item.title: Contract killers found guilty of murdering underworld figure Gavin Preston
item.description: A jury has found Jaeden Tito and Rabii Zahabe guilty of murdering underworld figure Gavin Preston outside a Melbourne cafe in 2023.
item.dc:creator: Kristian Silva

media:group.media:description: Underworld figure Gavin Preston was shot dead outside a Keilor cafe in Melbourne's north-west in September 2023.

item.title: Why there is $3b to be found beyond Sydney's tourism icons
item.description: A new report from urban think tank Committee for Sydney suggests expanding the city's brand to highlight its cultural hubs and festivals in order to attract investment.
item.dc:creator: Declan Bowring

media:group.media:description: Sydney needs to expand its brand identity beyond the iconic harbour, a new report argues.

item.title: Seeds of discontent with major parties could bloom in by-election
item.description: The Coalition has held the seat of Farrer for more than 70 years, but voters sense that could change after Saturday's by-election.
item.dc:creator: Nicola Ceccato

media:group.media:description: Graham Nott has decorated his van to support One Nation candidate David Farley.

item.title: Israeli money launderer found guilty in Sydney investment scam trial
item.description: Israeli citizen Shabtay Yaacoby is found guilty of laundering more than $10 million stolen by investment scammers.
item.dc:creator: Julian Fell

media:group.media:description: The jury found Shabtay Yaacoby was knowingly working with fraudsters.

item.title: 'Scared for my life': Jewish teacher feared Mardi Gras attack
item.description: The man told the Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion he was embraced by the LGBTQIA+ community when he came out as gay as a teenager, but the experience was markedly different when he told loved ones of his religious conversion.
item.dc:creator: Phoebe Pin

media:group.media:description: The man who gave evidence, not pictured, said the Jewish group Dayenu marched in Sydney's Mardi Gras parade with a heavy police presence.

item.title: E-bike rider dies after crash with orange bus in Perth's south
item.description: A 37-year-old man has died after the electric bicycle he was riding hit a bus in Parmelia on Thursday afternoon.

media:group.media:description: Officers from the Major Crash unit are investigating the fatal crash and have examined the crash scene.

item.title: Is this the most trees facing the axe in Adelaide Parklands since settlement?
item.description: More than 1,000 trees are scheduled to be chopped down in Adelaide's Parklands if all the South Australian government's projects go ahead as planned.
item.dc:creator: Malcolm Sutton

media:group.media:description: Protests have been growing against the premier's plans to fell parklands trees.

item.title: Julie Bishop bows to pressure, resigning as ANU chancellor
item.description: The former foreign minister had previously rejected calls from university staff and students to resign.
item.dc:creator: Monte Bovill

media:group.media:description: Julie Bishop was the university's 13th chancellor and had held the role since 2020.

item.title: Statement from mother of Kumanjayi Little Baby read at Alice Springs vigil
item.description: At a vigil held in Alice Springs last night for Kumanjayi Little Baby, a statement was read on behalf of her mother who called for the community to remember her five-year-old child as a "pretty girl in pink" who was "truly loved" by her family.
item.dc:creator: Grace Atta, Alex Barwick, and James Elton

media:group.media:description: Alice Springs residents gathered at ANZAC Oval for a vigil in honour of Kumanjayi Little Baby.
